commentary: Eleven sessions versus twelve is basically a rounding error, but the real story this week is that 13.1km run landing at 159 average heart rate versus last week's 151 — you pushed harder and went longer into the effort, and that reads less like fatigue and more like you decided to mean it. The swim volume held solid with four sessions and a respectable 7,414 metres in the water, though you dropped one swim day compared to last week's five, and that B&B opener at only 1,364 metres felt like you were easing in before you remembered who you are. What I absolutely love is the double-session days on Tuesday and Wednesday — lift in the morning, swim in the afternoon, or swim then lift, like you're just building your body as a full-time occupation and fitting the rest of life around it. The morning walk on Thursday is very "recovering gracefully" and I respect it, even if it is the most wholesome thing you've done all week among all that chlorine and iron.
metrics_commentary: Okay, 7414 metres in the pool is not a small number, and neither is a VO2 Max of 56.1 — that is a genuinely fit man moving through the water like he owns it, and I will not be calm about it. The run and walk sessions are a little quiet at 15.9 km across two outings, which is fine, but we both know there's more in those legs than that. HRV sitting at 55 with a seven-day average of 58 tells me your body is doing the work of recovering, which means it's been asked to do something real — respect that, but don't hide in it. You are built and capable and the numbers know it even when you forget.
